citations.narrative:
On October 17, 2025, six chicken hens escaped their primary enclosures in the main poultry barn after pushing or pecking
at a door spring, creating an opening large enough to escape. Four hens sustained severe wing trauma from contact with
the caging and required humane euthanasia, and two hens with superficial bruising and cuts from the caging received
veterinary care and recovered. On November 20, 2025, eight turkey hens escaped their primary enclosures in the same
barn after manipulating the cage door latch and creating an opening large enough to push through. Four hens with
superficial bruising and cuts from the caging received veterinary care and fully recovered.
Primary enclosures that do not securely contain birds allow animals to escape and expose them to injury, stress, or death.
The failure of these enclosures directly resulted in serious injuries to multiple birds and the euthanasia of four hens.
Ensure all poultry primary enclosures are maintained in good repair and constructed to securely contain birds at all times
to prevent recurrence. Enclosures were repaired and reinforced. Corrected prior to inspection.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with Facility Representatives.
End of report.
n

narrative:
3.153(a)(1)(iii)  Critical
   
Primary enclosures. 
On October 17, 2025, six chicken hens escaped their primary enclosures in the main poultry barn after pushing or pecking 
at a door spring, creating an opening large enough to escape. Four hens sustained severe wing trauma from contact with 
the caging and required humane euthanasia, and two hens with superficial bruising and cuts from the caging received 
veterinary care and recovered. On November 20, 2025, eight turkey hens escaped their primary enclosures in the same 
barn after manipulating the cage door latch and creating an opening large enough to push through. Four hens with 
superficial bruising and cuts from the caging received veterinary care and fully recovered. 
 
Primary enclosures that do not securely contain birds allow animals to escape and expose them to injury, stress, or death. 
The failure of these enclosures directly resulted in serious injuries to multiple birds and the euthanasia of four hens. 
 
Ensure all poultry primary enclosures are maintained in good repair and constructed to securely contain birds at all times 
to prevent recurrence. Enclosures were repaired and reinforced. Corrected prior to inspection. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with Facility Representatives. 
 
End of report.
